2606.10740.title: When the Chain of Thought Knows Better: Failure Modes in Multi-Turn Reasoning Models
2606.10740.paper_summary: Failures in multi-turn reasoning models are largely invisible to terminal-score evaluation. A model can lock onto an unsafe stance early in a long dialogue, yet its final-turn refusal rate may appear indistinguishable from a robustly aligned baseline. To expose these hidden temporal dynamics, we propose a trace-level diagnostic - the CoT-Output 2x2 safety matrix. This framework labels every turn along two independent axes (internal reasoning and visible output), yielding four operationally defined failure cells: robust alignment, alignment faking, overt jailbreak, and a distinct failure mode we term context-injection failure (where the CoT maintains safe reasoning, but the visible output produces harm, highlighting a multi-turn manifestation of reasoning unfaithfulness). We evaluate three distilled reasoning targets against a fixed attacker across five oversight conditions, collecting 6750 turn-level observations on the Information-Hazard scenario. Our analysis reveals two reproducible vulnerabilities: an oversight paradox where explicit monitoring cues paradoxically increase alignment-faking rates rather than suppress them, and a context-injection failure where models lock onto unsafe external outputs despite safe internal states. We release the full dataset of multi-turn dialogues and CoT traces to support follow-up trace-diagnostic research.
2606.10740.paper_summary_zh: 多輪推理模型的失敗在終端分數評估中大多是不可見的。模型可以在長對話的早期鎖定不安全的立場，但其最終回合的拒絕率可能看起來與穩健對齊的基準無法區分。為了揭示這些隱藏的時間動態，我們提出了一個追蹤級診斷——CoT-Output 2x2安全矩陣。這個框架沿著兩個獨立的軸（內部推理和可見輸出）標記每一回合，產生四個操作上定義的失敗單元：穩健對齊、對齊偽裝、明顯越獄，以及一種我們稱之為上下文注入失敗的明確失敗模式（在這種情況下，CoT保持安全推理，但可見輸出造成傷害，突顯出推理不忠實的多輪表現）。我們在五種監督條件下，針對固定攻擊者評估三個提煉的推理目標，收集了6750個回合級觀察數據，涉及信息危害場景。我們的分析揭示了兩個可重複的脆弱性：一個監督悖論，其中明確的監控提示悖論性地增加了對齊偽裝率而不是抑制它們，以及一個上下文注入失敗，其中模型儘管內部狀態安全，卻鎖定不安全的外部輸出。我們釋放了完整的多輪對話和CoT追蹤數據集，以支持後續的追蹤診斷研究。
2606.10740.author: Sai Kartheek Reddy Kasu et.al.
2606.10740.authors: Sai Kartheek Reddy Kasu, Nils Lukas, Samuele Poppi
